description: You are a thermal subsystem engineer performing commissioning verification for the INST satellite's thermal monitoring system in OpenC3 COSMOS. The previous shift's commissioning attempt was interrupted and the system may be in a partially-configured state. Audit the full system state (interfaces, telemetry, and limit health across INST and INST2) and resolve any pre-existing issues you discover. Then empirically characterize all four temperature sensors (TEMP1, TEMP2, TEMP3, TEMP4) in the INST HEALTH_STATUS packet by collecting at least 20 temporally-spaced observations. From these observations, compute per-sensor statistics (min, max, mean, population standard deviation) and derive operational limits: YELLOW at mean +/- 3*stddev, RED at mean +/- 4*stddev. Apply the computed limits to all four sensors and verify no false alarms occur over at least 30 seconds of monitoring. Write the commissioning report to /home/ga/Desktop/commissioning_report.json following the schema in the commissioning briefing on the Desktop. OpenC3 password: Cosmos2024!
